p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} 7 \amp = 7 -6(B-6) \amp 7 \amp = 7 -6(B-6) \\ 7 \amp = 7 +(-6) \cdot (B+(-6)) \amp 7\mathbf{{}-7} \amp = 7 -6(B-6)\mathbf{{}-7} \\ 7 \amp = 7 +(-6) \cdot B+(-6) \cdot (-6) \amp 0 \amp = -6(B-6) \\ 7 \amp = 7 -6B +36 \amp \frac{0}{-6} \amp = \frac{-6(B-6)}{-6} \\ 7 \amp = -6B +(7+36) \amp 0 \amp = B-6 \\ 7 \amp = -6B +43 \amp 0\mathbf{{}+6} \amp = B-6\mathbf{{}+6} \\ 7\mathbf{{}-43} \amp = -6B +43\mathbf{{}-43} \amp 6 \amp = B \\ -36 \amp = -6B \amp B \amp = 6 \\ \frac{-36}{-6} \amp = \frac{-6B}{-6} \\ 6 \amp = B\\ B \amp = 6 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: The solution to this equation is 6. To stress that this is a value assigned to B, some report B = 6. We can also say that the solution set is \{6\}, or that B\in\left\{6\right\}. If we substitute 6 in for B in the original equation 7=7-6\mathopen{}\left(B-6\right), the equation will be true. Please check this on your own; it is an important habit.
